seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with LangGraph enables sophisticated, stateful workflows where agents analyze codebases, generate fixes, execute in sandboxes, and iterate through debugging cycles. However, the complexity of cyclical graphs and parallel state management introduces risks like infinite loops, state corruption, and security vulnerabilities in generated code. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code correctness, security, and compliance at critical checkpoints.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant is structured as a LangGraph workflow with nodes for repository analysis, code generation, sandbox execution, test validation, and iterative debugging. State is maintained across cycles, with parallel branches for security scanning and license checks that merge back into the main flow. Human-in-the-loop approval gates ensure fixes are reviewed before deployment.

Key nodes include: RepoAnalyzer (loads context), CodeGenerator (LLM-powered fixes), SandboxExecutor (safe execution), TestValidator (pytest runner), and SecurityScanner (vulnerability checks). Conditional edges handle iteration until tests pass or max cycles are reached.

Checkpoints persist state across sessions, enabling long-running debugging sessions with human intervention.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: Debugging loops without proper termination conditions can trap the agent in endless code-fix cycles
risks.mitigation: Add Vex verification with cycle limits and convergence checks before state transitions

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: Security scan and test runner producing incompatible state updates during merge
risks.mitigation: Use Vex to validate state schema consistency before checkpoint writes

risks.name: Checkpoint deserialization errors after schema changes
risks.description: Evolving code generation schemas break persisted checkpoints from previous sessions
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation ensures backward compatibility during state hydration

risks.name: Human-in-the-loop breakpoints bypassed by conditional edges
risks.description: Dynamic routing skips critical human review when tests falsely pass
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces mandatory verification gates regardless of conditional logic

implementation.title: Basic LangGraph Coding Assistant Workflow
implementation.description: Core graph structure with repo analysis, code generation, and test execution nodes
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver

class CodeState(TypedDict):
    repo_path: str
    bug_description: str
    generated_code: str
    test_results: dict
    cycle_count: Annotated[int, "add"]

    def repo_analyzer(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
        # Load repo context and identify bug
        return {"generated_code": "", "cycle_count": 0}

    def code_generator(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
        # LLM generates fix
        return {"generated_code": "def fix_bug(x): return x * 2"}

    def test_runner(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
        # Execute pytest
        return {"test_results": {"passed": False}}

graph = StateGraph(CodeState)
graph.add_node("analyze", repo_analyzer)
graph.add_node("generate", code_generator)
graph.add_node("test", test_runner)
graph.set_entry_point("analyze")
graph.add_edge("analyze", "generate")
graph.add_conditional_edges("test", lambda s: "generate" if not s["test_results"]["passed"] else END)
checkpointer = SqliteSaver.from_conn_string(":memory:")
app = graph.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Node
implementation.description: Secure execution environment for generated code with resource limits
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os
from pathlib import Path

def sandbox_executor(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        code_path = Path(tmpdir) / "fix.py"
        code_path.write_text(state["generated_code"])
        
        result = subprocess.run([
            "docker", "run", "--rm", "-v", f"{tmpdir}:/app", 
            "python:3.11-slim", 
            "python", "/app/fix.py"
        ], capture_output=True, timeout=30)
        
    return {"execution_result": result.stdout.decode() if result.returncode == 0 else result.stderr.decode()}

# Add to graph
graph.add_node("sandbox", sandbox_executor)
graph.add_edge("generate", "sandbox")

implementation.title: Parallel Security and Test Validation
implementation.description: Fan-out to security scanning and testing with state merge
implementation.code:
from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode, tools_condition

def security_scanner(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    # Bandit security scan
    result = subprocess.run(["bandit", "-r", state["repo_path"]], capture_output=True)
    return {"security_issues": result.stdout.decode()}

def merge_results(state: CodeState) -> str:
    if state.get("security_issues") or not state["test_results"]["passed"]:
        return "retry"
    return "approve"

# Parallel execution
graph.add_node("security", security_scanner)
graph.add_node("validate", test_runner)
graph.add_edge("sandbox", ["security", "validate"])
graph.add_conditional_edges("validate", merge_results, {"retry": "generate", "approve": END})

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Review Node
implementation.description: Conditional human approval with configurable breakpoints

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Global verification of final outputs ensuring code safety and correctness
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Risk profile for coding assistant
policy = {
    "risk_profile": "medium",
    "verifications": ["code_correctness", "security_vulnerability_scanning", "no_credential_leakage", "dependency_safety", "license_compliance"]
}

def verified_invoke(inputs: dict, config: dict):
    result = app.invoke(inputs, config)
    
    # Verify final generated code
    verification = vex_client.verify(
        result["generated_code"],
        policy,
        context={
            "repo_path": inputs["repo_path"],
            "language": "python",
            "use_case": "ai-coding-assistant"
        }
    )
    
    if not verification.passed:
        raise ValueError(f"Vex verification failed: {verification.issues}")
    
    return result

# Usage
result = verified_invoke({"repo_path": "/app", "bug_description": "fix auth bug"}, config)

vexIntegration.description: Replace custom security scanner with Vex-powered verification node

vexIntegration.title: State Schema Validation Before Checkpoints
vexIntegration.description: Prevent corruption by validating state before persistence

cta.heading: Secure Your LangGraph AI Coding Assistant
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect your coding agents from security risks and infinite loops.
